paper_title: Discrepancy of arithmetic progressions in grids
summary: The conjecture that the lower bound in Theorem 1.3 is tight up to a constant factor (i.e., no logarithmic factor) remains open. Li and Nikolov (2025, arXiv:2504.12598) improved the upper bound gap from a factor of log|Omega_N|/(log log|Omega_N|) to sqrt(log|Omega_N|), using factorization norms, but explicitly state that removing this final sqrt(log) factor is beyond their methods. The conjecture itself is restated as open in that paper.

since_posted.title: Discrepancy of Arithmetic Progressions in Boxes and Convex Bodies
since_posted.claim: Improves the upper bound for disc(A_N) from a log|Omega_N|/(log log|Omega_N|) factor above the conjectured tight bound to a sqrt(log|Omega_N|) factor, via factorization norms; the tight constant-factor conjecture remains explicitly open.

search_queries: discrepancy arithmetic progressions grids Fox Xu Zhou 2110.15429 tight upper bound
discrepancy arithmetic progressions d-dimensional grid upper bound logarithmic factor
notes: The source paper was published in Mathematika (2024). The main open question is whether the sqrt(log) gap in arXiv:2504.12598 can be removed to match the lower bound by a constant depending only on d.
